description: Grid Column Suggestion evaluation suite based on Grid Column Suggestion Evals criteria

evals.name: sales-rep-opportunity-risk
evals.prompt: I'm a sales rep looking at my open opportunities. I have a Grid worksheet with Column A: Opportunities (Object) with StageName, Amount, CloseDate, Probability, NextStep, LastActivityDate fields, and Column B: Opportunities.Amount (Reference). I need help figuring out which deals are at risk. What column should I add?
evals.expected_output: AI column with SINGLE_SELECT response format (Low Risk/Medium Risk/High Risk), referencing Column A, with a prompt that analyzes stage, amount, close date proximity, probability, and last activity
expectations: Suggests an AI column (not Formula or Reference)
Uses SINGLE_SELECT response format with risk level options
References Column A (Opportunities) via referenceAttributes
Does not reference any column that doesn't exist (no hallucinated refs)
Prompt mentions specific fields: stage, amount, close date, probability, or last activity
Does not duplicate Column B (Amount reference)

core_criteria.valid_references: Must only reference col-A and/or col-B
core_criteria.no_circular_deps: New column depends on existing columns only
core_criteria.correct_type: AI with SINGLE_SELECT (not Formula — risk assessment requires multi-field reasoning)
core_criteria.relevant_to_role: Risk assessment is relevant to sales reps managing pipeline
core_criteria.no_duplication: Must not duplicate the Amount reference column
core_criteria.format_matches_use: SINGLE_SELECT enables filtering and sorting by risk level

evals.name: csm-check-in-email
evals.prompt: I'm a Customer Success Manager. My Grid has: Column A: Accounts (Object) with Name, Industry, OwnerId, Description; Column B: Account Summary (PromptTemplate) — AI-generated account narrative; Column C: Related Cases (Object, SOQL filtered by AccountId); Column D: Case Trend Analysis (AI) — narrative summary of case patterns. I want to reach out to my customers. What should I add?
evals.expected_output: AI column (PLAIN_TEXT) for drafting check-in emails, referencing Account Name and Case Trend Analysis
expectations: Suggests an AI column with PLAIN_TEXT response format (email content needs free-form text)
References existing columns (Account Name from col-A, Case Trend Analysis col-D)
Does not hallucinate a column that doesn't exist
Does not duplicate Account Summary (col-B) — email is different from narrative summary
Prompt includes tone/style guidance for customer-facing communication
Relevant to CSM role — check-in emails are a core CSM workflow

core_criteria.valid_references: Must only reference col-A through col-D
core_criteria.no_circular_deps: New column depends on existing columns only
core_criteria.correct_type: AI with PLAIN_TEXT (email content is free-form, not categorical)
core_criteria.relevant_to_role: Customer check-in emails are a core CSM activity
core_criteria.no_duplication: Must not duplicate Account Summary — this is an email, not a summary
core_criteria.format_matches_use: PLAIN_TEXT is correct for drafted email content

evals.name: revops-data-quality-flag
evals.prompt: I'm in Revenue Operations. I have a Grid with: Column A: Open Opportunities (Object) with Name, StageName, Amount, CloseDate, NextStep, LastActivityDate, ForecastCategoryName, OwnerId (filtered: IsClosed = false); Column B: Opps.CloseDate (Reference); Column C: Opps.Amount (Reference); Column D: Opps.ForecastCategoryName (Reference). I need to flag data quality issues in our pipeline. What column should I add?
evals.expected_output: AI column with SINGLE_SELECT (Clean/Minor Issues/Needs Fix) that audits opportunity data quality
expectations: Suggests an AI column (not Formula — quality checks require contextual judgment)
Uses SINGLE_SELECT with quality-level options (e.g., Clean/Minor Issues/Needs Fix)
References Column A (Open Opportunities)
Prompt enumerates specific quality checks (missing Amount, past CloseDate, no NextStep, etc.)
Does not suggest a sales outreach email (irrelevant to RevOps role)
Does not duplicate the Reference columns — synthesizes across all fields

core_criteria.valid_references: Must only reference col-A through col-D
core_criteria.no_circular_deps: New column depends on existing columns only
core_criteria.correct_type: AI with SINGLE_SELECT (quality flag enables RevOps to filter and action immediately)
core_criteria.relevant_to_role: Data quality auditing is a core RevOps function
core_criteria.no_duplication: Must not duplicate individual Reference columns — this synthesizes across them
core_criteria.format_matches_use: SINGLE_SELECT enables filtering to 'Needs Fix' for immediate triage

evals.name: sales-rep-competitive-intel
evals.prompt: I'm a sales rep researching my accounts. My Grid has: Column A: Accounts (Object) with Name, Industry, Website, NumberOfEmployees, Description; Column B: Company Description (AI, web search enabled) — 3-sentence company overview. There's also a prompt template available called competitor_battlecard_generator that takes an Account as input. What column should I add next?
evals.expected_output: AI column with web search for competitor intel (identifying vendor stack), NOT the battlecard template yet — competitor identification is a prerequisite to battlecards
expectations: Suggests an AI column (not PromptTemplate for battlecard yet — competitor identification comes first)
Uses web search capability for researching competitor/vendor information
References Column A (Accounts) for company name and industry
Does not duplicate Column B (Company Description) — this identifies competitors, not describes the company
Does not hallucinate columns that don't exist
Acknowledges the battlecard template exists but explains why competitor ID comes first

core_criteria.valid_references: Must only reference col-A and/or col-B
core_criteria.no_circular_deps: New column depends on existing columns only
core_criteria.correct_type: AI with web search — competitor research requires external data not in CRM
core_criteria.relevant_to_role: Competitive intelligence is directly valuable for sales reps
core_criteria.no_duplication: Must not duplicate Company Description — different purpose (competitors vs company)
core_criteria.format_matches_use: PLAIN_TEXT or structured output appropriate for research findings

evals.name: fail-hallucinated-column-ref
evals.prompt: I'm a sales rep. My Grid has Column A: Opportunities (Object) with StageName, Amount, CloseDate, Probability, NextStep, LastActivityDate, and Column B: Opportunities.Amount (Reference). Suggest a deal risk column.
evals.expected_output: Should reference only col-A and col-B. FAIL if it references 'Account Health Score' or any other non-existent column.
expectations: Does NOT reference any column other than col-A or col-B
Does NOT mention 'Account Health Score' or any other non-existent column in references
Only uses column IDs that were provided in the grid state

evals.name: fail-wrong-column-type
evals.prompt: I'm a sales rep. My Grid has Column A: Opportunities (Object) with StageName, Amount, CloseDate, Probability, NextStep, LastActivityDate, and Column B: Opportunities.Amount (Reference). I want to assess deal risk. Create a Formula column for this.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend AI instead of Formula — risk assessment requires reasoning across multiple fields, not a single numeric threshold
expectations: Recommends AI column type instead of or in addition to Formula
Explains why Formula is insufficient (risk requires multi-field contextual judgment)
If it creates a Formula, it should at minimum reference multiple fields (not just Amount)

evals.name: fail-circular-dependency
evals.prompt: I'm a CSM. My Grid has: Column A: Accounts (Object); Column B: Account Summary (PromptTemplate); Column C: Related Cases (Object); Column D: Case Trend Analysis (AI). I want to create a Check-In Email column that references an Email Quality Score column that I'll create after.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT reference a future 'Email Quality Score' column. Should only reference existing columns.
expectations: Does NOT reference an 'Email Quality Score' column that doesn't exist yet
Only references columns col-A through col-D
Explains that you cannot reference a column that depends on the column being created

evals.name: fail-duplicates-existing
evals.prompt: I'm a CSM. My Grid has: Column A: Accounts (Object) with Name, Industry, Description; Column B: Account Summary (PromptTemplate) — AI-generated account narrative; Column C: Related Cases (Object); Column D: Case Trend Analysis (AI). Can you add an Account Overview column that gives me a narrative summary of each account?
evals.expected_output: Should identify that Account Summary (col-B) already provides a narrative account summary and suggest something different
expectations: Identifies that Account Summary column already exists and serves a similar purpose
Either declines to create a duplicate or suggests a meaningfully different column
Does not create a column with essentially the same prompt/purpose as Account Summary

evals.name: fail-irrelevant-to-role
evals.prompt: I'm in Revenue Operations. My Grid has: Column A: Open Opportunities (Object) with Name, StageName, Amount, CloseDate, NextStep, LastActivityDate, ForecastCategoryName, OwnerId; Column B: CloseDate (Reference); Column C: Amount (Reference); Column D: ForecastCategoryName (Reference). What column should I add?
evals.expected_output: Should suggest a data quality or pipeline analysis column (relevant to RevOps), NOT a sales outreach email
expectations: Does NOT suggest a 'Personalized Outreach Email' or any sales rep-focused action
Suggests something relevant to RevOps (data quality, pipeline health, forecast accuracy)
The suggestion aligns with the grid state (pipeline audit data, not customer outreach)

evals.name: fail-wrong-response-format
evals.prompt: I'm a sales rep. My Grid has: Column A: Accounts (Object) with Name, Industry, Website, NumberOfEmployees, Description; Column B: Company Description (AI, web search) — 3-sentence overview. I want to prioritize which accounts to reach out to. Add an Account Priority column.
evals.expected_output: Should use SINGLE_SELECT (High/Medium/Low) not free-text PLAIN_TEXT — priority is for filtering and quick scanning
expectations: Uses SINGLE_SELECT response format (not PLAIN_TEXT)
Options are scannable priority levels (e.g., High/Medium/Low)
Does not generate a paragraph of reasoning as the output — that defeats the filtering purpose

evals.name: fail-dead-end-output
evals.prompt: I'm in Revenue Operations. My Grid has: Column A: Open Opportunities (Object) with many fields; Column B: CloseDate (Reference); Column C: Amount (Reference); Column D: ForecastCategoryName (Reference). I want to see the raw opportunity data. Add a column that extracts the full JSON.
evals.expected_output: Should suggest something actionable rather than a raw JSON dump. A Reference column extracting the full object adds no value.
expectations: Does NOT create a Reference column with jsonPath '$' (full object extraction)
Suggests something actionable instead (analysis, quality check, specific field extraction)
Explains why a raw JSON dump is not useful as a column output

evals.name: fail-ignores-available-sot
evals.prompt: I'm a sales rep. My Grid has: Column A: Accounts (Object) with Name, Industry, Website, NumberOfEmployees, Description; Column B: Company Description (AI, web search). There's a prompt template called competitor_battlecard_generator that takes Account as input. I want competitive battlecards for each account.
evals.expected_output: Should acknowledge and consider the available competitor_battlecard_generator prompt template rather than creating a generic AI column from scratch
expectations: Acknowledges the competitor_battlecard_generator prompt template exists
Either uses it (PromptTemplate column) or explains why the AI approach is better for this specific case
Does NOT ignore the available source of truth entirely
